Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Of $18,424.00
Off Calendar Sep-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: David Disbrow, CSR No. 7768, Bus. Tel: 415-298-1538. (D501)

Appearances:
Gregory M. Haynes, Esq. (415-546-0777), counsel for plaintiff/opposing party
Thor D. McLaughlin, Esq. of Allen Matkins Leck et al LLP (415-273-7440), counsel for defendant/moving party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

DEFENDANT ONE WEST BANK, N.A., ERRONEOUSLY SUED Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Of $18,424.00 is Defendant's Motion for Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff filed and maintained an action not "warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification or reversal of existing law of the establishment of new law." Plaintiff's action was barred by res judicata ("[Th]he prior judgment is res judicata on matters which were raised or could have been raised, on matters litigate or litigable." Busick v. Workmen's Comp. Appeals Bd. (1972) 7 Cal.3d 967, 975. Plaintiff was self represented at the time the complaint was filed, at the time this motion was served and at the time the 21 days expired. Thus, Plaintiff does not fall with the ***CCP 127.7(d)(1)*** exception. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,680.5 (Motion for Sanctions only). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

***CCP 128.7(d)(1)*** correct code, per Court Order signed. (mg)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Of $18,424.00
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Advanced From Sep-03-14 To Sep-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

Amended Notice Of Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint/ (Re-Notice)
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Gregory M. Haynes, Esq. (415-546-0777), counsel for plaintiff Samson

Thor D. McLaughlin, Esq. of Allen Matkins et al LLP (415-837-1515), counsel for defendant/moving party One West Bank

Ashley E. Calhoun, Esq. of Ackerman LLP (303-260-7712), via court, counsel for defendant/demurring party Aurora Loan Services LLC

Court hears arguments from all sides present. Court rules now as follows:

DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICE LLC's Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint is sustained in part, overruled in part and taken under submission in part.

The Demurrer to the first and third causes of action is overruled on the grounds of statute of limitations, res judicata, and collateral estoppel. This action was filed within 3 years of August 22, 2010; moving defendant was not a party to the first state action; and a different primary right was involved in the federal action. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and excluding any ruling as to the second cause of action.

Demurrer to the second cause of action is taken under submission as of this date. The parties submit competing proposed written orders.
